skill.name: preclinical-pkpd-analyst
skill.description: Calculate pharmacokinetic parameters from preclinical concentration-time data using non-compartmental analysis and compartmental modeling.
skill.summary: Automated PK/PD analysis for preclinical drug studies with structured workflows and reproducible output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 44 static analysis findings are false positives. The 27 Ruby/shell backtick execution alarms are markdown code formatting in documentation, not actual shell command substitution. The 5 weak cryptographic algorithm alerts match JSON field text and argparse code, not cryptographic operations. The path traversal and system reconnaissance findings refer to security documentation and audit report text. The skill performs legitimate Python script execution and file I/O as part of its PK/PD data analysis function. No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 5 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. All are false positives: 2 match JSON text in an audit report file, 1 matches argparse setup code in scripts/main.py:71, and 2 match YAML front matter and documentation text in SKILL.md. No cryptographic operations exist in this skill.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 27 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All are markdown formatting backticks used for code spans and fenced code blocks in documentation. No actual shell command substitution using backticks occurs. The markdown backticks surround example commands like 'python scripts/main.py' and 'pip install -r requirements.txt'.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 2 instances of 'Path traversal sequence' in SKILL.md. Lines 115 and 118 mention '../' inside a Security Checklist section describing what to prevent, not actual path traversal code. The text reads 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)' as security documentation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 8 instances of 'System reconnaissance' across audit result JSON and SKILL.md. All match descriptive text in audit reports and documentation. No actual reconnaissance commands or system enumeration present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 2 instances of 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7. This is a single GitHub source repository link in documentation ('https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills'), not an exfiltration or malicious network call. The URL is a legitimate citation of the skill source.

content.user_title: Run Preclinical PK/PD Analysis with Structured Workflows
content.value_statement: Researchers need reliable pharmacokinetic analysis from preclinical concentration-time data but often lack a reproducible, documented pipeline. This skill automates PK parameter calculation using non-compartmental and compartmental modeling with explicit assumptions, bounded scope, and clear output boundaries.
actual_capabilities: Calculate key PK parameters: AUC, Cmax, Tmax, half-life, clearance, and volume of distribution
Run one-compartment oral PK model fitting using scipy curve fitting
Process concentration-time data from CSV files with automated trapezoidal AUC calculation
Generate structured PK reports with assumptions, risks, and unresolved items documented
Run demo analysis with built-in example data for quick validation
limitations: Only supports one-compartment oral PK model, not multi-compartment or IV bolus models
Does not support population PK modeling or mixed-effects analysis
No statistical comparison between dose groups or treatment arms
Cannot process data from non-standard concentration-time formats without manual preprocessing

use_cases.title: IND-Enabling PK Study Analysis
use_cases.description: Calculate PK parameters from rat or mouse concentration-time data to support Investigational New Drug applications with reproducible results.

use_cases.title: Drug Candidate Ranking
use_cases.description: Compare PK profiles across multiple drug candidates to identify the most promising compound based on AUC, half-life, and clearance metrics.
use_cases.target_user: Drug discovery scientist evaluating lead compounds

use_cases.title: WinNonlin Alternative for Quick Analysis
use_cases.description: Perform non-compartmental analysis as a free, scriptable alternative to commercial PK software for rapid internal assessments.
use_cases.target_user: Research scientist needing rapid PK parameter estimates

prompt_templates.title: Basic PK Parameter Calculation from CSV Data
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a CSV file at data/pk_concentrations.csv with columns 'time' (hours) and 'conc' (ng/mL). Use the preclinical-pkpd-analyst skill to calculate AUC, Cmax, Tmax, and half-life from this data. The dose was 10 mg/kg oral administration in rats.
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard PK analysis from user-provided concentration-time data file

prompt_templates.title: Demo Run for Workflow Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the preclinical-pkpd-analyst skill in demo mode to validate the analysis pipeline. Show me the expected PK parameters from the built-in example data and explain what each parameter means.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user verifying skill setup and understanding PK output

prompt_templates.title: Comparative PK Analysis Across Doses
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three CSV files for a dose escalation study: data/dose_low.csv (1 mg/kg), data/dose_mid.csv (3 mg/kg), and data/dose_high.csv (10 mg/kg) in rats. Each file has time (h) and concentration (ng/mL) columns. Use the preclinical-pkpd-analyst skill to calculate PK parameters for each dose level and present them in a comparison table. Note that dose-normalized comparisons are not supported - report raw parameters only.
prompt_templates.scenario: Dose proportionality assessment across multiple dose groups

prompt_templates.title: PK Analysis with Structured Output Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the preclinical-pkpd-analyst skill to analyze the PK data from data/study_01.csv (time in hours, concentration in ug/mL). The administration was oral gavage at 5 mg/kg in mice. I need the results in the structured format with sections for Objective, Inputs Used, Assumptions, Workflow, Deliverable, Risks and Limits, and Next Checks. Document any fitting convergence issues explicitly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user requiring full audit trail and documented assumptions for regulatory context

output_examples.input: Rat PK data at 10 mg/kg oral dose - time points: 0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 24 hours with concentrations 0, 5.2, 8.5, 7.8, 5.1, 2.8, 1.5, 0.4 ug/mL
output: Assumptions: One-compartment oral model, linear elimination, dose-normalized data

output_examples.input: Run demo analysis on the built-in example PK dataset
output: Note: Demo uses fixed example data. Replace with --data flag for real analysis.

best_practices: Always validate input data format: CSV with comma-separated time and concentration columns, no headers required but consistency is essential.
Document assumptions explicitly in the output: note the PK model used, any data excluded, and whether dose normalization was applied.
Run python -m py_compile scripts/main.py before full execution to catch syntax errors early in the process.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for human clinical PK data or bioequivalence studies - it is designed for preclinical rodent data only.
Do not skip the structured output format for complex requests - the assumptions and risks sections are critical for scientific reproducibility.
Do not modify the curve fitting parameters without documenting the change - default p0 values assume typical oral absorption profiles.

faq.question: What PK parameters does this skill calculate?
faq.answer: The skill calculates Cmax, Tmax, AUC (trapezoidal rule), elimination half-life (t1/2), elimination rate constant (ke), absorption rate constant (ka), and clearance.

faq.question: What data format does the skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts CSV files with two columns: time points and corresponding concentrations, separated by commas. No header row is required.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for human clinical data?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for preclinical rodent PK analysis. Human clinical data requires different models, allometric scaling, and regulatory considerations.

faq.question: What PK model does the analysis use?
faq.answer: The skill uses a one-compartment oral absorption model with first-order elimination. It fits parameters A, ke (elimination), and ka (absorption) using scipy curve fitting.

faq.question: Does this skill make network calls or send data externally?
faq.answer: No. The skill runs entirely locally. It reads CSV files from your workspace and writes output to the console. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: What happens if the curve fit does not converge?
faq.answer: The scipy curve_fit function may fail on noisy or sparse data. If fitting fails, the skill reports the error and the workflow instructs users to provide a manual fallback analysis.
